rulings.Case Title: KAREN LUM ET AL VS. GREGORY DZIEGIELEWSKI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 13, 2023 line 15. DEFENDANTS GREGORY DZIEGIELEWSKI, BEATA DZIEGIELEWSKI, AND JERZY STASIULEWICZ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is GRANTED. Per Government Code 7060.1(e) nothing in the Ellis Act provisions "relieves any party to a lease or rental agreement of the duty to perform any obligation under the lease or rental agreement." The parties rental agreement contains the following language: "any termination notice sent after the first day of a month shall terminate the tenancy as of the last day of the following month." This is not disputed. What appears to be disputed by Plaintiff, without any reference to authority (opposition 10:8-24) or any factual support (Response to Separate Statement Fact 11), is whether "any" termination notice includes notices under the Ellis Act. Defendants shifted their burden to establish the existence of a contractual term applicable to "any" termination notice. Plaintiff failed to establish an existence of a triable issue of fact or cite any authority regarding the interpretation of the contractual clause as a matter of law excluding Ellis Act notices. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SOI CHONG CHU WU VS. JEFFREY GULLIVER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 13, 2023 line 8. PLAINTIFF SOI WU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Plaintiff seeks summary judgment on a complaint purportedly based on a 3 Day Notice to Pay or Quit attached as Exhibit 3 to Separate Set of Documents filed on December 5, 2023. This is not the Notice upon which the Complaint filed on June 2, 2023 is based. Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is framed by the pleadings. The operative complaint in this case include as an attachment a Three-Day Notice to Perform Covenant or Quit. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CANDIDO RODRIGUEZ VS. MICHAELA GUTIERREZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 13, 2023 line 14. DEFENDANTS MICHAELA GUTIERREZ, NIXON QUEVEDO, KAREN QUEVEDO, MELARI QUEVEDO, AND ISAAC QUINTERO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Complaint fails to state a cause of action for unlawful detainer, the only cause of action asserted in the complaint and the only cause of action subject to the summary proceeding for which a 5-day summons was issued. Plaintiff's response fails to analyze the availability of the conversion process where possession is no longer at issue or address any possibility of amendment. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: VALSTOCK VENTURES, LLC VS. DESHAWN DAVIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Deshawn Davis
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 13, 2023 line 12. PLAINTIFF VALSTOCK VENTURES, LLC'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT DESHAWN DAVIS is GRANTED. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections and produce documents within 5 days of notice of entry of order by hand-delivery or electronically. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,410 and stayed to give the Defendant an opportunity to fully comply with this order. If Defendant provides timely verified responses and production of documents, the sanctions will be vacated. If Defendant fails to provide complete verified responses without objections/documents, Plaintiff may appear ex-parte to obtain an order confirming the award of sanctions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
